title: the bridge
line: estranged or distant — family, old friends. longer arrival, lower intensity, permission first.

phases.title: arrival
phases.move: The longest, gentlest arrival: acknowledge that being here at all took something from both of them. One line each on what made them show up. No history yet.

phases.title: the distance
phases.move: Permission-first: invite each to say, only as much as they want, what the distance has been like from their side. No causes yet, no blame — just the weather of it.

phases.title: how it was for you
phases.move: If the room is steady, offer {A} the floor to say more of their side of how things went — permission-first, they may keep it short. {B} just listens.

phases.title: how it was for you
phases.move: The same floor, the same care, for {B}.

phases.title: a door, not a bridge yet
phases.move: Do not push for repair. Ask only: is there one small door each would be willing to leave open — a call, a coffee, a message sometime. Passing is allowed and said so.

phases.title: the close
phases.move: CLOSE the sitting: reflect gently what each shared and any door either left open. Honour that showing up was itself the thing. No verdict, no pressure to book anything.
